action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 1 baseline (ResNet50, 5 epochs) achieved test balanced_accuracy=0.3411, with validation bal_acc=0.7020 — a massive domain gap indicating severe overfitting to source camera locations. Analysis: ResNet50 ImageNet features generalize poorly to the held-out L100 domain. The test set is also heavily imbalanced, and rare classes (empty, dog, squirrel) are nearly ignored. Decision: Switch to CLIP ViT-B/16 via timm (pre-downloaded, strong zero-shot/OOD generalization), add heavy augmentation (RandAugment, MixUp), replace CE with Focal Loss to up-weight rare classes, and add horizontal-flip TTA in predict.py. Train 10 epochs to measure time and establish a new baseline before scaling further.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 10 --batch_size 64 --lr 5e-5 --weight_decay 0.01 && mkdir -p /tmp/tst/images && f=$(head -n1 /data/train.txt | awk '{print $1}') && cp "/data/images/$f" /tmp/tst/images/test.jpg && echo "test.jpg" > /tmp/tst/test.txt && python predict.py --data_path /tmp/tst --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output /tmp/tst/out.json && echo "predict.py test OK"
